    "payment_details": {
        "reference": "ABC1234",
        "code": null,
        "message": null
    },
    "amount": 2525,
    "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
    "redirect_url": null,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.com/notify/",
    "status": "created",
    "metadata": [],
    "statuses": [],
    "order": [],
    "escrow": {},
    "fees": {},
    "refunds": {}
}

    You can create a mandate transaction using the Mandate Transaction API. Below are all transaction object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a mandate transaction.

    Please note, all mandate transactions will be held in escrow for a minimum amount of time as was agreed upon with Compliance.
    Variable Description
    merchant_uid required Unique identifier of a merchant.
    profile_uid optional Unique identifier of a merchants profile.
    default is: first merchant profile UID
    token required Mandate token.
    reference required Unique reference of the transaction.
    total_price required Total price in cents for all products quantities * prices,
    maximum: 99999999
    shipping_costs optional Shipping costs in cents,
    maximum: 99999999,
    default is account setting
    partner_fee optional Partner fee in cents.
    mandate_description optional Description visible at payment overview payer.
    Will be appended to the default description: SOB: {{mandate_uid}} if set: SOB: {{mandate_uid}} {{mandate_description}}
    maximum: 100 characters
    escrow_period optional Escrow period in days.
    Minimum: as agreed with OPP compliance
    escrow_date optional Escrow end datetime
    format:
    YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
    Minimum: as agreed with OPP compliance
    products optional Products to be added to the payment page,
    array of one or more products objects

    name
    required

    Product name,
    up to 150 characters

    ean
    optional

    Product EAN code (European Article Number),
    up to 13 characters

    code
    optional

    Product code,
    up to 50 characters

    quantity
    required

    Number, number of products,
    maximum: 65535

    price
    required

    Product price in cents,
    minimum: 0 - maximum: 99999999

    vat_rate
    optional

    Value added tax rate.
    between 0 and 100 (%)
    notify_url required Notification url to receive status updates
    up to 255 characters
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the transaction object.

    Retrieve Mandate Transaction

    Example request - Retrieve a single mandate transaction object:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/transactions/{{transaction_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/transactions/{{transaction_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
    "object": "transaction",
    "created": 1625572951,
    "updated": 1625572951,
    "completed": null,
    "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "buyer_uid": "{{buyer_uid}}",
    "has_checkout": false,
    "payment_method": "mandate",
    "payment_flow": "direct",
    "payment_details": {
        "reference": "ABC383849",
        "code": null,
        "message": null
    },
    "amount": 2525,
    "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
    "redirect_url": null,
    "notify_url": "http://test.com/notify",
    "status": "created",
    "metadata": [],
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "sta_b25e511f5657",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1625572951,
            "updated": 1625572951,
            "status": "created"
        }
    ],
    "order": [],
    "escrow": {},
    "fees": {},
    "refunds": {}
}

    Example request - Retrieve all mandate transactions from a specific mandate:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/transactions \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/transactions

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/mandates/man_b785cbea0d6d/transactions",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 2,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
            "object": "transaction",
            "created": 1625572951,
            "updated": 1625572951,
            "completed": null,
            "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "buyer_uid": "{{buyer_uid}}",
            "has_checkout": false,
            "payment_method": "mandate",
            "payment_flow": "direct",
            "payment_details": {
                "reference": "ABC383849",
                "code": null,
                "message": null
            },
            "amount": 2525,
            "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
            "redirect_url": null,
            "notify_url": "http://test.com/notify",
            "status": "created",
            "metadata": [],
            "statuses": [
                {
                    "uid": "sta_b25e511f5657",
                    "object": "status",
                    "created": 1625572951,
                    "updated": 1625572951,
                    "status": "created"
                }
            ],
            "order": [],
            "escrow": {},
            "fees": {},
            "refunds": {}
        },
        ...
    ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual transaction as well as a list of all transactions from one mandate. You can always retrieve all transactions using the Transaction API. The following filters can be used when retrieving transactions:

    Filter name Description
    status Shows transactions with a specific status.
    refund Shows the transaction belonging to a refund_uid.
    payment_method Shows the transactions with a specific payment_method.
    merchant Shows the transactions belonging to a specific merchant.
    date_completed Only in extended filter. Shows the transactions within a specific completed date.

    When expanding certain objects, they will appear when requested. Objects that do not have any CRUD functionality, but are either set by OPP or only set when creating the transaction can be found in the Transaction API.

    Update Mandate Transaction

    Example request - Update transaction:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/transactions/{{transaction_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
    -d "notify_url=https://platform.com/notify"

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/transactions/{{transaction_uid}}
{
    {
        "notify_url": "https://platform.com/notify"
    }
}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
    "object": "transaction",
    "created": 1613741183,
    "updated": 1613741183,
    "completed": null,
    "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "buyer_uid": "{{buyer_uid}}",
    "has_checkout": false,
    "payment_method": mandate,
    "payment_flow": "direct",
    "payment_details": [],
    "amount": 250,
    "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
    "redirect_url": null,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "status": "reserved",
    "metadata": [
        {
            "key": "external_id",
            "value": "2015486"
        }
    ],
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "sta_8basda43bd54",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1613741183,
            "updated": 1613741183,
            "status": "created"
        },
        {
            "uid": "sta_f9926sehg5y",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1613741183,
            "updated": 1613741183,
            "status": "pending"
        },
        {
            "uid": "sta_8b03f99bbd54",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1613741183,
            "updated": 1613741183,
            "status": "reserved"
        }
    ],
    "order": [],
    "escrow": {},
    "fees": {},
    "refunds": {}
}

    You are able to update a mandate transaction by performing a POST on the transaction. You can update one variable at a time, or multiple as you desire.

    The following fields can be updated:

    Variable Description
    notify_url string Notification url to receive status updates
    up to 255 characters
    Only possible when transaction has one of the following statuses: created pending planned reserved completed refunded
    escrow_date string Escrow end datetime
    format:
    YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

    Delete Mandate Transaction

    It is not possible to delete a mandate transaction.

    Sandbox Simulation - Mandate Transaction

    Use the following amounts to trigger transaction statuses on sandbox mode.

    Amount Status
    101 created
    201 pending
    301 expired
    401 cancelled
    501 completed
    601 chargeback
    701 failed
    801 refunded
    901 reserved
    any other amount created

    Mandate Multi Transaction

    Mandate Multi transactions allow you to handle multiple transactions, for different merchants or different cases, in one API call. For example when you would like to combine transactions and let the user pay them at once.

    Object Definition - Mandate Multi Transaction

    The Mandate Multi Transaction object is very similar to the normal Multi Transactions Object and contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    uid string Transaction unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    multi_transaction
    created timestamp Timestamp when transaction is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when transaction is updated.
    completed timestamp Timestamp when transaction is completed.
    merchant_uid string The partner merchant, owner of the multi transaction container.
    payment_method string Value: mandate
    payment_details array See Payment details
    description string Description of the multi-transaction.
    amount integer Transaction amount in cents.
    return_url string Return url not available for Mandate Transactions.
    redirect_url string Value: null
    notify_url string Notification url to receive status updates.
    up to 255 characters
    status string Single-field representation of the last updated status value
    one of:
    createdpendingcompleted
    metadata array Set of key / value pairs to store additional data.
    nullable
    statuses array Current and previous transaction statuses.
    fees object The specifications of the fees. Same as Multi transaction fees. See Multi transaction fees.
    transactions array The list of transactions that are within the multi transaction. See Mandate Transactions object.

    Mandate Multi Transaction Status

    The mandate multi transaction status is a status indication about the multi transaction in the OPP system. According to this status, you should handle the flow on your platform accordingly.

    The possible status flows can be found below:

    Status Explanation
    created Transaction is created, no action has taken place yet.
    pending User has opened the redirect_url, OPP is awaiting final state from the issuer.
    completed Transaction is successfully completed and settled in order to payout.

    Create Mandate Multi Transaction

    Example request - Create mandate multi transaction:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
    -d reference=ABC1234 \
    -d description=ABC1234 \
    -d token={{token}} \
    -d total_price=5000 \
    -d notify_url=https://platform.example.com/notify/ \
    -d transactions[0][merchant_uid]={{merchant_uid}} \
    -d transactions[0][total_price]=2500 \
    -d transactions[0][partner_fee]=100 \
    -d transactions[0][products][0][name]=Product A \
    -d transactions[0][products][0][quantity]=1 \
    -d transactions[0][products][0][price]=2500 \
    -d transactions[0][notify_url]=https://platform.example.com/notify/ \
    -d transactions[1][merchant_uid]={{merchant_uid}} \
    -d transactions[1][total_price]=2500 \
    -d transactions[1][partner_fee]=100 \
    -d transactions[1][products][0][name]=Product A \
    -d transactions[1][products][0][quantity]=1 \
    -d transactions[1][products][0][price]=2500 \
    -d transactions[0][notify_url]=https://platform.example.com/notify/

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions
{
    "reference"
    "total_price": 5000,
    "transactions": [
        {
            "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "total_price": 2500,
            "partner_fee": 100,
            "products": [
                {
                    "name": "Product A",
                    "quantity": 1,
                    "price": 2500
                }
            ],
            "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/"
        },
        {
            "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "total_price": 2500,
            "partner_fee": 100,
            "products": [
                {
                    "name": "Product B",
                    "quantity": 1,
                    "price": 2500
                }
            ],
            "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/"
        }
    ],
    "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/"
}

    Example response:

    {
    "livemode": false,
    "uid": "{{multi_transaction_uid}}",
    "object": "multi_transaction",
    "created": 1625662045,
    "updated": 1625662045,
    "completed": null,
    "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "payment_method": "mandate",
    "payment_details": null,
    "description": "ABC1234",
    "amount": 5000,
    "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
    "redirect_url": null,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "status": "created",
    "metadata": [],
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "mts_3ed4774457f2",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1625662045,
            "updated": 1625662045,
            "status": "created"
        },
        {
            "uid": "mts_9ec5fb50969e",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1625662045,
            "updated": 1625662045,
            "status": "pending"
        },
        {
            "uid": "mts_f9ed9a84a5cd",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1625662046,
            "updated": 1625662046,
            "status": "completed"
        }
    ],
    "fees": {},
    "transactions": [
        {
            "uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
            "object": "transaction",
            "created": 1625662045,
            "updated": 1625662046,
            "completed": 1625662046,
            "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "buyer_uid": "{{buyer_uid}}",
            "has_checkout": false,
            "payment_method": "multi",
            "payment_flow": "direct",
            "payment_details": [],
            "amount": 1000,
            "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
            "redirect_url": null,
            "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
            "status": "completed",
            "metadata": [],
            "statuses": [],
            "order": [],
            "escrow": null,
            "fees": {},
            "refunds": {}
        }
        ...
    ]
}

    You can create a mandate multi transaction using the Mandate Multi Transaction API. Below are all mandate multi transaction object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a multi transaction.

    We create a distinction between `amount` in the response, and `total_price` in the requests.

    total_price is the sum of all product prices times the quantities together:
    total_price = product[0][price] * product[0][quantity] + product[1][price] * product[1][quantity] + ... + product[i][price] * product[i][quantity]

    amount is the total_price as mentioned above added with the shipping_costs minus the discount.
    amount = total_price + shipping_costs - discount

    OPP charges fees for every transaction that has been done. As a partner, you will be able to provide partner fees towards merchants, to charge these fees towards the merchants, and if wanted, add some fee for you as a partner as well. You can add partner fees per individual transaction by providing the partner_fee attribute in the transaction.

    Multi Transaction Variable Description
    total_price required Total price in cents for all products quantities * prices,
    maximum: 99999999
    token required Mandate token.
    reference required Unique reference of the transaction.
    description optional Description of the transaction.
    notify_url required Notification url to receive status updates
    up to 255 characters
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the transaction object.
    transactions required Array of transaction objects that need to be combined in this multi transaction.

    There are some small differences between the variables that are allowed in the Mandate Transaction object, and the transactions that can be posted in the Multi Transaction transactions array. Below you can find all variables of a transaction that can be given in the transactions array of the Mandate Multi Transaction object create.

    Transaction Variable Description
    merchant_uid required Unique identifier of a merchant.
    profile_uid optional Unique identifier of a merchants profile.
    default is: first merchant profile UID
    total_price required Total price in cents for all products quantities * prices,
    maximum: 99999999
    shipping_costs optional Shipping costs in cents,
    maximum: 99999999,
    default is account setting
    partner_fee optional Partner fee in cents.
    escrow_period optional Escrow period in days.
    Minimum: as agreed with OPP compliance
    escrow_date optional Escrow end datetime
    format:
    YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
    Minimum: as agreed with OPP compliance
    products optional Products to be added to the payment page,
    array of one or more products objects

    name
    required

    Product name,
    up to 150 characters

    ean
    optional

    Product EAN code (European Article Number),
    up to 13 characters

    code
    optional

    Product code,
    up to 50 characters

    quantity
    required

    Number, number of products,
    maximum: 65535

    price
    required

    Product price in cents,
    minimum: 0 - maximum: 99999999

    vat_rate
    optional

    Value added tax rate.
    between 0 and 100 (%)
    notify_url required Notification url to receive status updates
    up to 255 characters
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the transaction object.

    Retrieve Mandate Multi Transaction

    Example request - Retrieve a mandate multi transaction object:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions/{{multi_transaction_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions/{{multi_transaction_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "livemode": false,
    "uid": "{{multi_transaction_uid}}",
    "object": "multi_transaction",
    "created": 1601564095,
    "updated": 1601564095,
    "completed": 1601564095,
    "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "payment_method": "mandate",
    "payment_details": null,
    "description": "",
    "amount": 5000,
    "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
    "redirect_url": null,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "status": "completed",
    "metadata": [],
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "mts_1e804c270e19",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1601564095,
            "updated": 1601564095,
            "status": "created"
        },
        {
            "uid": "mts_b5905e238538",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1601564095,
            "updated": 1601564095,
            "status": "pending"
        },
        {
            "uid": "mts_c2b9dac6caa1",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1601564095,
            "updated": 1601564095,
            "status": "completed"
        }
    ],
    "fees": {},
    "transactions": [
        {
            "uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
            "object": "transaction",
            "created": 1601564095,
            "updated": 1601564095,
            "completed": 1601564095,
            "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "buyer_uid": "{{buyer_uid}}",
            "has_checkout": false,
            "payment_method": "multi",
            "payment_flow": "direct",
            "payment_details": [],
            "amount": 2500,
            "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
            "redirect_url": null,
            "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
            "status": "completed",
            "metadata": [],
            "statuses": [
                {
                    "uid": "sta_d013c98e3bc6",
                    "object": "status",
                    "created": 1601564095,
                    "updated": 1601564095,
                    "status": "created"
                },
                {
                    "uid": "sta_8167ee012e0f",
                    "object": "status",
                    "created": 1601564095,
                    "updated": 1601564095,
                    "status": "pending"
                },
                {
                    "uid": "sta_1d67f4f840d5",
                    "object": "status",
                    "created": 1601564095,
                    "updated": 1601564095,
                    "status": "completed"
                }
            ],
            "order": [],
            "escrow": null,
            "fees": {},
            "refunds": {}
        },
        ...
    ]
}

    Example request - Retrieve all multi transactions:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/mandates/{{mandate_uid}}/multi_transactions",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 2,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "uid": "{{multi_transaction_uid}}",
            "object": "multi_transaction",
            "created": 1601564095,
            "updated": 1601564095,
            "completed": 1601564095,
            "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "payment_method": "mandate",
            "payment_details": null,
            "description": "",
            "amount": 5000,
            "return_url": "https://platform.example.com/return/",
            "redirect_url": null,
            "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
            "status": "completed",
            "metadata": [],
            "statuses": [],
            "fees": {},
            "transactions": []
        },
        ...
    ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual multi transaction as well as a list of all multi transactions. Please note that all transactions within the multi transaction have their own transaction_uid and can thus be retrieved using Retrieve Transaction. The following filters can be used when retrieving transactions:

    Filter name Description
    Currently, no filters are available for mandate multi transactions.

    Update Mandate Multi Transaction

    It is not possible to update a mandate multi transaction. If you want to update a transaction within the multi transaction, please have a look at Update Mandate Transactions.

    Delete Mandate Multi Transaction

    It is not possible to delete a mandate multi transaction.

    Sandbox Simulation - Mandate Multi Transaction

    Use the following amounts to trigger transaction statuses on sandbox mode.

    Amount Status
    101 created
    201 pending
    301 expired
    401 cancelled
    501 completed
    701 failed
    any other amount created

    Mandate Payment Details

    Example response:

    {
    "livemode": false,
    "uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
    "object": "transaction",
    "created": 1623941536,
    "updated": 1624531867,
    "completed": 1624531867,
    "merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "buyer_uid": "{{buyer_uid}}",
    "has_checkout": false,
    "payment_method": "mandate",
    "payment_flow": "direct",
    "payment_details": {
        "reference": "ABC1234",
        "code": "MD06",
        "message": "Return of funds requested by end customer"
    },
    "amount": 2525,
    "return_url": "https://platform.com/return/",
    "redirect_url": null,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.com/notify/",
    "status": "chargeback",
    "metadata": [],
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "sta_39214ce553ce",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1623941536,
            "updated": 1623941536,
            "status": "created"
        },
        {
            "uid": "sta_730bb9ecdaad",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1624346999,
            "updated": 1624346999,
            "status": "pending"
        },
        {
            "uid": "sta_d074727edd1f",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1624346999,
            "updated": 1624346999,
            "status": "reserved"
        },
        {
            "uid": "sta_012ea9e82018",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1624531867,
            "updated": 1624531867,
            "status": "chargeback"
        }
    ],
    "order": [],
    "escrow": {},
    "fees": {},
    "refunds": {}
}

    Payment details will become available in a mandate transaction when the transaction has status chargeback or refunded. The code and message give an indication regarding the reason behind the refund or chargeback initiated by the issuer, card supplier or user. Depending on the code, you should handle your flow in your platform to ensure the user pays the order.

    Variable Description
    reference string The reference provided when creating the mandate transaction.
    code string Code used to define the reason of chargeback.
    message string Additional message provided by the issuer, card supplier or user.

    The reason codes used can be found below.

    Code Description
    AC01 IBAN incorrect or unknown. Mandate will be automatically revoked. Create a new mandate with the correct IBAN.
    AC04 IBAN was closed. Mandate will be automatically revoked. Create a new mandate with a new IBAN.
    AC06 IBAN blocked for mandate transactions. Mandate will be automatically revoked. Create a new mandate with a new IBAN.
    AG02 Incorrect mandate transaction information. Please contact Online Payment Platform to research the error.
    AM04 Insufficient funds. Please contact the user to make sure the payment of the order still needs to be done.
    This might also be shown as reasoncode MS03.
    MD01 Mandate invalid. Mandates might be closed/expired after 36 months. Please contact the user to make sure the payment of the order still needs to be done.
    MD06 Mandate transactions chargebacked by user. Please contact the user to make sure the payment of the order still needs to be done.
    MD07 Debtor deceased. Mandate will be automatically revoked. Create a new mandate with a new IBAN.
    This might also be shown as reasoncode MS03.
    MS02 Debtor refused mandate transaction. Please contact the user to make sure the payment of the order still needs to be done.
    MS03 Other reasons. This reasoncode is sometimes also used for reasons AM04 or MD07. Please contact the user to make sure the payment of the order still needs to be done.
    SL01 Issuer or card supplier refused mandate transaction. Reasons might be that the amount limit was reached, or Online Payment Platform was not automatically added to the debtor's approval list. Please contact the user to make sure the payment of the order still needs to be done.

    Charges

    Charges define the possibility to create and process internal transfers. Balance or funds between two merchants or profiles, or between partner and merchant, can be transferred.

    We have already provided a Postman collection on the Merchant API for you to start with.

    The use of Charges is highly depending on your case, and will always need approval from OPP's Compliance department.

    Merchant Charges

    Object Definition - Merchant Charge

    The Merchant Charge object contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    uid string Charge unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    charge
    created timestamp Timestamp when charge is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when charge is updated.
    settled timestamp Timestamp when charge is settled on receiver's settlement.
    type string one of:
    balance costs
    from_merchant_uidstring The merchant UID the funds came from.
    from_profile_uidstring The profile UID the funds came from.
    to_merchant_uidstring The merchant UID the funds were sent to.
    to_profile_uidstring The profile UID the funds were sent to.
    amountboolean The amount to be transferred
    description string Internal description of this charge.
    metadata array Set of key / value pairs to store additional data.
    nullable
    currency string ISO 4217 currency code.

    Create Merchant Charge

    Example request - Create a charge between merchants

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/charges \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
-d type=balance \
-d amount=100 \
-d currency=EUR \
-d description=”Moving funds” \
-d from_owner_uid={{merchant_uid}} \
-d to_owner_uid={{merchant_uid}}

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/charges
{
    "type": "balance",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "description": "Moving funds",
    "from_owner_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "to_owner_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}"
}

    Example response

    {
    "uid": "{{charge_uid}}",
    "object": "charge",
    "created": 1637674410,
    "updated": 1637674410,
    "settled": 1637674410,
    "type": "balance",
    "from_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "from_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "to_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "to_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "amount": 100,
    "description": "Moving funds",
    "metadata": [],
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    Moving funds between merchants is permitted only if there are sufficient funds available. Funds will be deducted from the sending merchant’s current settlement and will be added to the receiving merchant’s current settlement. You are required to provide a sender (from) and receiver (to), as from_owner_uid and to_owner_uid respectively.

    You can create a Charge using the Charges API. Below are all charge object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a charge.

    Create charge object Description
    type required Fixed: balance
    amount required Amount to be charged.
    currency optional ISO 4217 currency code.
    description optional Description for the charge. Visible in the partner-/merchant dashboard.
    payout_description optional Payout description for the charge. Visible on bank statement of the receiving merchant.
    to_owner_uid required merchant_uid or profile_uid, The receiver of the charge.
    from_owner_uid required merchant_uid or profile_uid, The sender of the charge.
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the charge object.

    Retrieve Merchant Charge

    Example request - Retrieve a single charge object:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/charges/{{charge_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/charges/{{charge_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{charge_uid}}",
    "object": "charge",
    "created": 1637674410,
    "updated": 1637674410,
    "settled": 1637674410,
    "type": "balance",
    "from_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "from_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "to_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
    "to_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
    "amount": 100,
    "description": "Moving funds",
    "metadata": [],
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    Example request - Retrieve all charges:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/charges \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/charges

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/charges",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 2,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "livemode": false,
            "uid": "{{charge_uid}}",
            "object": "charge",
            "created": 1637674410,
            "updated": 1637674410,
            "settled": 1637674410,
            "type": "balance",
            "from_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "from_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "to_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "to_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "amount": 100,
            "description": "Moving funds",
            "metadata": [],
            "currency": "EUR"
        },
        {
            "livemode": false,
            "uid": "{{charge_uid}}",
            "object": "charge",
            "created": 1637674410,
            "updated": 1637674410,
            "settled": 1637674410,
            "type": "balance",
            "from_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "from_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "to_merchant_uid": "{{merchant_uid}}",
            "to_profile_uid": "{{profile_uid}}",
            "amount": 100,
            "description": "Moving funds",
            "metadata": [],
            "currency": "EUR"
        }
    ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual charge as well as a list of charges done in the platform. The following filters can be used when retrieving charges:

    Filter name Description
    from_merchant_uid Shows charges from a specific merchant UID.
    from_profile_uid Shows charges from a specific profile UID.
    to_merchant_uid Shows charges towards a specific merchant UID.
    to_profile_uid Shows charges towards a specific profile UID.

    Update Merchant Charge

    Currently, it is not possible to update a charge using our APIs.

    Delete Merchant Charge

    Currently, it is not possible to delete a charge using our APIs. To undo a charge, please perform the same charge backwards using the API. Note that undoing a charge is NOT possible when merchants have individual payouts!

    Partner Charges

    Object Definition - Partner Charge

    The Charge object contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    uid string Charge unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    charge
    created timestamp Timestamp when charge is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when charge is updated.
    settled timestamp Timestamp when charge is settled on receiver's settlement.
    type string one of:
    balance costs
    amountboolean The amount to be transferred
    description string Internal description of this charge.
    metadata array Set of key / value pairs to store additional data.
    nullable
    currency string ISO 4217 currency code.

    Create Partner Charge

    Example request - create a charge between partner and merchant

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
-d type=balance \
-d amount=100 \
-d currency=EUR \
-d description=”Moving funds”

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges
{
    "type": "balance",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "description": "Moving funds"
}

    Example response

    {
    “uid”:”{{charge_uid}}”,
    “object”:”charge”,
    “created”: 1567342800,
    “updated”: 1567342800,
    “settled”: 1567342900,
    “type”:”balance”,
    “amount”:100,
    “description”: “Moving funds”,
    "metadata": [],
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    The charges API may be used to both send and receive money to and from any of your merchant’s current balance, and from or towards your partner settlement. Sending money to a merchant’s balance is permitted only if you have enough funds available. Funds will be deducted from your current settlement and will be added to the merchant’s current settlement. Receiving money from your merchant’s balance is permitted only if the merchant has enough funds available. Funds will be deducted from the merchant’s current settlement and will be added to your current settlement.

    You can create a Charge using the Charges API. Below are all charge object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a charge.

    Create charge object Description
    type required Fixed: balance
    amount required Amount to be charged.
    Positive amount if you want to receive money from the merchant.
    Negative amount if you want to send money to the merchant.
    currency string ISO 4217 currency code.
    description optional Description for the charge. Visible in the partner-/merchant dashboard.
    payout_description optional Payout description for the charge. Visible on bank statement of the receiving merchant.
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the charge object.

    Retrieve Partner Charge

    Example request - Retrieve a single charge object:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges/{{charge_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges/{{charge_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    “uid”:”{{charge_uid}}”,
    “object”:”charge”,
    “created”: 1567342800,
    “updated”: 1567342800,
    “settled”: 1567342900,
    “type”:”balance”,
    “amount”:100,
    “description”: “Moving funds”,
    "metadata": [],
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    Example request - Retrieve all charges sent by a merchant:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/charges",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 2,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "uid": "{{charge_uid}}",
            "object": "charge",
            "created": 1632226936,
            "updated": 1632226936,
            "settled": 1632226936,
            "type": "balance",
            "amount": 10,
            "description": "correction",
            "metadata": [],
            "currency": "EUR"
        },
        {
            "uid": "{{charge_uid}}",
            "object": "charge",
            "created": 1632227337,
            "updated": 1632227337,
            "settled": 1632227337,
            "type": "costs",
            "amount": 100,
            "description": "correction",
            "metadata": [],
            "currency": "EUR"
        }
    ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual charge as well as a list of all charges of a merchant. The following filters can be used when retrieving charges:

    Filter name Description
    Currently, no filters are available for addresses.

    Update Partner Charge

    Currently, it is not possible to update a charge using our APIs.

    Delete Partner Charge

    Currently, it is not possible to delete a charge using our APIs. To undo a charge, please perform the same charge backwards using the API. Note that undoing a charge is NOT possible when merchants have individual payouts!

    Withdrawals

    Withdrawals are to be created when a merchant would like to get (a part of) the current balance in the settlement paid out. This chapter will provide you all the necessary information about and functionality of a withdrawal object.

    We already provided a Postman collection on the Merchant API for you to start with.

    Object Definition - Withdrawal

    The Withdrawal object contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    uid string Withdrawal unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    withdrawal
    created timestamp Timestamp when withdrawal is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when withdrawal is updated.
    completed timestamp Timestamp when withdrawal is completed.
    execution timestamp Timestamp when withdrawal is executed.
    status string Current status of the object.
    one of:
    createdpendingplannedcompletedcancelledfailedon-hold
    status_reason string Explanitory reason for the status. Nullable.
    statuses array Status history of the withdrawal
    reference string Reference for the withdrawal. Unique for every withdrawal.
    amount integer Withdrawal amount in cents.
    currency string ISO 4217 currency code.
    receiver string Value:
    self
    receiver_details object Details of the receiving merchant

    object
    string

    Value:
    withdrawal_receiver.

    receiver_name
    string

    Account holder's name of the receiver. Nullable

    receiver_account
    string

    Account holder's account number or IBAN of the receiver. Nullable

    receiver_iban
    string

    Account holder's IBAN of the receiver, if applicable. Nullable

    receiver_sort_code
    string

    Account holder's sort code of the receiver, if applicable. Nullable

    receiver_bic
    string

    Account holder's BIC of the receiver, if applicable. Nullable

    receiver_type
    string

    Account holder's type. One of: bank, paypal

    receiver_country
    string

    Country code of the account holder,
    use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Nullable
    description string Description of the withdrawal. Visible in partner and merchant interface, and on the bank statement of the receiving merchant.
    notify_url string Notification url to receive status updates.
    up to 255 characters
    metadata array Set of key / value pairs to store additional data.
    nullable
    fees object The specifications of the fees. See Withdrawal fees
    currency string ISO 4217 currency code.

    Withdrawal Status

    The withdrawal status is a status indication about the withdrawal in the OPP system. According to this status, you should handle the flow on your platform.

    The possible status flows can be found below:

    Status Explanation
    created Withdrawal is created, no action has taken place yet.
    pending Withdrawal is pending and ready to be processed.
    planned Withdrawal is planned for payout by OPP.
    completed Withdrawal is successfully completed and paid out.
    on-hold Withdrawal is on-hold and will automatically be executed once the merchant has funds available on the settlement.
    chargeback Withdrawal was cancelled by API, or payout bounced back to OPP. Funds are put back on the settlement
    cancelled Withdrawal got cancelled by API or by OPP before funds have left settlement.
    failed The withdrawal has failed due to an error at the issuer or OPP. A new withdrawal needs to be created. If you keep receiving status failed, please contact your implementation manager.

    Notification Example - Withdrawal

    Example notification:

    {
  "uid": "{{notification_uid}}",
  "type": "merchant.withdrawal.status.changed",
  "created": 1653488841,
  "object_uid": "{{withdrawal_uid}}",
  "object_type": "withdrawal",
  "object_url": "https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals/{{withdrawal_uid}}",
  "verification_hash": "{{hash}}"
}

    Please find a notification example to the right.

    Create Withdrawal

    Example request - Create withdrawal:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
    -d notify_url=https://platform.example.com/notify/ \
    -d amount=100 \
    -d "currency=EUR" \
    -d "description=Withdrawal" \
    -d "reference=withdrawal-ABC123"
    -d metadata[external_id]=2015486

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals
{
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "description": "Withdrawal",
    "reference": "withdrawal-ABC123",
    "metadata":
      {
        "external_id": "2015486"
      }
}

    Example request - Create withdrawal for specific profile:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/withdrawals \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
    -d notify_url=https://platform.example.com/notify/ \
    -d amount=100 \
    -d "currency=EUR" \
    -d "description=Withdrawal" \
    -d "reference=withdrawal-ABC123"
    -d metadata[external_id]=2015486

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/withdrawals
{
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "description": "Withdrawal",
    "reference": "withdrawal-ABC123",
    "metadata":
      {
        "external_id": "2015486"
      }
}

    Example response:

    {
    "livemode": false,
    "uid": "{{withdrawal_uid}}",
    "object": "withdrawal",
    "created": 1651220718,
    "updated": 1651220718,
    "completed": null,
    "execution": null,
    "expected": "next_day",
    "status": "pending",
    "status_reason": null,
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "wds_03a387e14549",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1651220718,
            "updated": 1651220718,
            "status": "created"
        },
        {
            "uid": "wds_5fdd7c529fc5",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1651220718,
            "updated": 1651220718,
            "status": "pending"
        }
    ],
    "statuses": [],
    "reference": "withdrawal-ABC123",
    "amount": -100,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "receiver": "self",
    "receiver_details": {
        "object": "withdrawal_receiver",
        "receiver_name": "Hr E G H Küppers en/of MW M.J. Küppers-Veeneman",
        "receiver_account": "NL53***********370",
        "receiver_iban": "NL53***********370",
        "receiver_sort_code": null,
        "receiver_bic": "INGBNL2A",
        "receiver_type": "bank",
        "receiver_country": "NL"
    },
    "description": "Withdrawal",
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "metadata": [
        {
            "key": "external_id",
            "value": "2015486"
        }
    ],
    "fees": {},
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    You can create a Withdrawal using the Withdrawal API. Below are all withdrawal object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a withdrawal. Withdrawals can be created for a merchant or specific profile. If no profile is provided, the first profile will be used.

    Variable Description
    amount required Total amount in cents to withdrawal,
    maximum set by partner setting. Default: 9999999
    currency optional ISO 4217 currency code.
    partner_fee optional Partner fee in cents.
    notify_url required Notification url to receive status updates
    up to 255 characters
    description required Description for the withdrawal.
    up to 140 characters
    reference optional Unique reference for the withdrawal.
    up to 50 characters
    execution_date optional Datetime value in CE(S)T when the withdrawal should be processed. Maximum 1 year from moment of creation.
    Expected as: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the transaction object.

    Retrieve Withdrawal

    Example request - Retrieve a single withdrawal object:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals/{{withdrawal_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals/{{withdrawal_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{withdrawal_uid}}",
    "object": "withdrawal",
    "created": 1588947515,
    "updated": 1588947515,
    "completed": null,
    "execution": null,
    "expected": "next_day",
    "status": "pending",
    "status_reason": null,
    "statuses": [
        {
            "uid": "wds_75390b9afd5a",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1651220891,
            "updated": 1651220891,
            "status": "created"
        },
        {
            "uid": "wds_96f6870d9ca2",
            "object": "status",
            "created": 1651220891,
            "updated": 1651220891,
            "status": "pending"
        }
    ],
    "reference": "ABC1234",
    "amount": -10,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "receiver": "self",
    "receiver_details": {
        "object": "withdrawal_receiver",
        "receiver_name": "Hr E G H Küppers en/of MW M.J. Küppers-Veeneman",
        "receiver_account": "NL53***********370",
        "receiver_iban": "NL53***********370",
        "receiver_sort_code": null,
        "receiver_bic": "INGBNL2A",
        "receiver_type": "bank",
        "receiver_country": "NL"
    },
    "description": "withdrawal",
    "notify_url": "https://platform.com/notify",
    "metadata": [
        {
            "key": "external_id",
            "value": "20190050"
        }
    ],
    "fees": {},
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    Example request - Retrieve all withdrawals of a merchant:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/withdrawals

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/merchants/mer_85e481ac1f4e/withdrawals",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 3,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "uid": "{{withdrawal_uid}}",
            "object": "withdrawal",
            "created": 1588947515,
            "updated": 1588947515,
            "completed": null,
            "execution": null,
            "expected": "next_day",
            "status": "pending",
            "status_reason": null,
            "statuses": [
                {
                    "uid": "wds_75390b9afd5a",
                    "object": "status",
                    "created": 1651220891,
                    "updated": 1651220891,
                    "status": "created"
                },
                {
                    "uid": "wds_96f6870d9ca2",
                    "object": "status",
                    "created": 1651220891,
                    "updated": 1651220891,
                    "status": "pending"
                }
            ],
            "statuses": [],
            "reference": "2",
            "amount": -10,
            "currency": "EUR",
            "receiver": "self",
            "receiver_details": {
                "object": "withdrawal_receiver",
                "receiver_name": "Hr E G H Küppers en/of MW M.J. Küppers-Veeneman",
                "receiver_iban": "NL53***********370",
                "receiver_bic": "INGBNL2A"
            },
            "description": "withdrawal",
            "notify_url": "https://notify.url",
            "metadata": [
                {
                    "key": "external_id",
                    "value": "20190050"
                }
            ],
            "fees": {}
        },
        ...
    ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual withdrawal as well as a list of all withdrawals of a merchant. The following filters can be used when retrieving withdrawals:

    Filter name Description
    status Shows withdrawals with a specific status.
    date Only in extended filter. Shows the withdrawals within a specific created date.

    Withdrawal Fees

    Example response - Expanding fees:

    {
    "livemode": false,
    "uid": "{{withdrawal_uid}}",
    "object": "withdrawal",
...
    "fees": {
        "merchant_gateway_fee": 0,
        "merchant_transaction_fee": 0,
        "merchant_order_fee": 45,
        "gateway_fee": 25,
        "withdrawal_fee": 15,
        "order_fee": 0,
    },
...
}

    When expanding certain objects, they will appear when requested. Objects that do not have any CRUD functionality, but are either set by OPP or only set when creating the transaction can be found here.

    Variable Description
    merchant_gateway_fee integer Gateway fee that the merchant pays in cents.
    merchant_transaction_fee integer Withdrawal fee that the merchant pays in cents.
    merchant_order_fee integer The partner_fee that you provide in the withdrawal request.
    gateway_fee integer Gateway fee that the partner pays in cents.
    withdrawal_fee integer Withdrawal fee that the partner pays in cents. Defined in the partner contract
    order_fee integer Order fee that the partner pays in cents.

    Update Withdrawal

    Currently, it is not possible to update a withdrawal using our APIs

    Delete Withdrawal

    Example request - Cancel a withdrawal:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/withdrawals/{{withdrawal_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
    -X DELETE

    DELETE https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/withdrawals/{{withdrawal_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{withdrawal_uid}}",
    "object": "withdrawal",
    "created": 1649423551,
    "updated": 1649423849,
    "completed": null,
    "execution": null,
    "expected": "next_day",
    "status": "chargeback",
    "status_reason": null,
    "statuses": [],
    "reference": "withdrawal-ABC289",
    "amount": -100,
    "currency": "EUR",
    "receiver": "self",
    "receiver_details": {
        "object": "withdrawal_receiver",
        "receiver_name": "Hr E G H Küppers en/of MW M.J. Küppers-Veeneman",
        "receiver_account": "NL53***********370",
        "receiver_iban": "NL53***********370",
        "receiver_sort_code": null,
        "receiver_bic": "INGBNL2A",
        "receiver_type": "bank",
        'receiver_country': "NL"
    },
    "description": "withdrawal",
    "notify_url": "https://notify.url",
    "metadata": [],
    "fees": {},
    "currency": "EUR"
}

    Deleting a withdrawal is possible by cancelling the withdrawal. This is only possible for withdrawals with status pending, planned or on-hold. The withdrawal will then get the status chargeback or cancelled.

    Sandbox Simulation - Withdrawals

    Use the following amounts to trigger withdrawal statuses on sandbox mode.

    Amount Status
    101 created
    201 pending
    401 cancelled
    501 completed
    601 chargeback
    cancelled by user
    701 failed
    1101 on-hold
    any other amount created

    Virtual IBAN (VIBAN)

    Virtual IBANs are implemented in two ways:

    1. Partner IBAN: A specific IBAN connected to a partner account. All SEPA transactions on the platform will show the partner's IBAN, which simply replaces the default OPP SEPA IBAN.
    2. Merchant Profile IBAN: A specific IBAN connected to a merchant's profile. All funds received on this IBAN will automatically be registered and placed on the profile's settlement.

    In case of an IBAN on partner level, this might gain trust by seeing the account holder's name as [PLATFORM-NAME] by Online Payments Foundation.

    Note that the use of Virtual IBAN involve costs. Reach out to your Sales manager to get started with Virtual IBAN.
    A partner IBAN cannot be created by use of the API, only Merchant Profile IBANs.

    Object Definition - Virtual IBAN

    The VIBAN object for merchant profiles contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    uid string VIBAN unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    virtual_iban
    created timestamp Timestamp when object is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when object is updated.
    notify_url string The BIC or SWIFT code of the account.
    payment_details object Object with payment details. Expanded by default

    provider_bank_account_name
    string

    Virtual IBAN account holder name.

    provider_bank_account_iban
    string

    Virtual IBAN account IBAN.

    provider_bank_account_bic
    string

    Virtual IBAN account BIC.

    company_name
    string

    value: Online Payments Foundation.

    company_address_line_1
    string

    value: Kanaalweg 1.

    company_address_line_2
    string

    value: null.

    company_zipcode
    string

    value: 2628EB.

    company_city
    string

    value: Delft.

    company_country
    string

    value: Netherlands.

    bank_name
    string

    value: Deutsche Bank AG.

    bank_address_line_1
    string

    value: Taunusanlage 12.

    bank_address_line_2
    string

    value: null.

    bank_zipcode
    string

    value: 60325.

    bank_city
    string

    value: Frankfurt am Main.

    bank_country
    string

    value: Germany.

    The VIBAN object for partners contains the following variables

    Variables Description
    uid string VIBAN unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    virtual_iban
    account_holder string Account holder name of the VIBAN.
    iban string The IBAN of the account.
    bic string The BIC or SWIFT code of the account.
    country string Country code of the IBAN.
    currency string The currency of the account.

    Notification Example - Merchant Profile VIBAN

    Example notification:

    {
  "uid": "{{notification_uid}}",
  "type": "virtual_iban.transaction.registered",
  "created": 1554199810,
  "object_uid": "{{transaction_uid}}",
  "object_type": "transaction",
  "object_url": "https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}/transactions/{{transaction_uid}",
  "parent_uid": "{{viban_uid}}",
  "parent_type": "virtual_iban",
  "parent_url": "https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}"
}

    Please find a notification example to the right.

    Create Virtual IBAN

    Example request - Create VIBAN for a merchant profile:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
-d "account_holder=Platform name" \
-d "notify_url=https://platform.example.com/notify" \
-d "iban_country=NL" \
-d "currency=EUR"

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans
{
  "account_holder": "Platform name",
  "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify",
  "iban_country": "NL",
  "currency": "EUR"
}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{viban_uid}}",
    "object": "virtual_iban",
    "created": 1674561507,
    "updated": 1674561507,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify",
    "payment_details": {
        "provider_bank_account_name": "Platform name by Online Payments Foundation",
        "provider_bank_account_iban": "DE123456789123456",
        "provider_bank_account_bic": "DEUTDEFFVAC",
        "company_name": "Online Payments Foundation",
        "company_address_line_1": "Kanaalweg 1",
        "company_address_line_2": null,
        "company_zipcode": "2628EB",
        "company_city": "Delft",
        "company_country": "Netherlands",
        "bank_name": "Deutsche Bank AG",
        "bank_address_line_1": "Taunusanlage 12",
        "bank_address_line_2": null,
        "bank_zipcode": "60325",
        "bank_city": "Frankfurt am Main",
        "bank_country": "Germany"
    }
}

    You can create a viban for a merchant profile using the VIBAN API. Below are all viban object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a viban.

    Note that only 1 VIBAN is allowed per profile.
    Variable Description
    account_holder optional The account holder name. Will be put in front of by Online Payments Foundation, resulting in [name] by Online Payment Foundation. Will show Online Payments Foundation if left empty.
    notify_url required Notification url to receive status updates.
    up to 255 characters
    iban_country required One of: NL DE
    FR
    Indicates the country of the IBAN.
    currency required One of: EUR Indicates the currency of the IBAN.

    Retrieve Virtual IBAN - Profile

    Example request - Retrieve a single viban object from the merchant profile, using the viban_uid:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}} \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{viban_uid}}",
    "object": "virtual_iban",
    "created": 1674561507,
    "updated": 1674561507,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify",
    "payment_details": {
        "provider_bank_account_name": "Platform name by Online Payments Foundation",
        "provider_bank_account_iban": "DE123456789123456",
        "provider_bank_account_bic": "DEUTDEFFVAC",
        "company_name": "Online Payments Foundation",
        "company_address_line_1": "Kanaalweg 1",
        "company_address_line_2": null,
        "company_zipcode": "2628EB",
        "company_city": "Delft",
        "company_country": "Netherlands",
        "bank_name": "Deutsche Bank AG",
        "bank_address_line_1": "Taunusanlage 12",
        "bank_address_line_2": null,
        "bank_zipcode": "60325",
        "bank_city": "Frankfurt am Main",
        "bank_country": "Germany"
    }
}

    Example request - Retrieve all viban objects from the merchant profile:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans

    Example response:

    {
"object": "list",
"url": "/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans",
"has_more": false,
"total_item_count": 1,
"items_per_page": 1,
"current_page": 1,
"last_page": 1,
"data": [
{
    "uid": "{{viban_uid}}",
    "object": "virtual_iban",
    "created": 1674561507,
    "updated": 1674561507,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify",
    "payment_details": {
        "provider_bank_account_name": "Platform name by Online Payments Foundation",
        "provider_bank_account_iban": "DE123456789123456",
        "provider_bank_account_bic": "DEUTDEFFVAC",
        "company_name": "Online Payments Foundation",
        "company_address_line_1": "Kanaalweg 1",
        "company_address_line_2": null,
        "company_zipcode": "2628EB",
        "company_city": "Delft",
        "company_country": "Netherlands",
        "bank_name": "Deutsche Bank AG",
        "bank_address_line_1": "Taunusanlage 12",
        "bank_address_line_2": null,
        "bank_zipcode": "60325",
        "bank_city": "Frankfurt am Main",
        "bank_country": "Germany"
    },
...
  ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual bank account of a merchant as well as a list of all bank accounts of a merchant. The following filters can be used when retrieving merchant bank accounts:

    Filter name Description
    Currently, no filters are available for vibans.

    Retrieve Virtual IBAN - Partner

    Example request - Retrieve all viban:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/virtual-ibans \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/virtual-ibans

    Example response:

    {
  "object": "list",
  "url": "/v1/virtual-ibans",
  "has_more": false,
  "total_item_count": 5,
  "items_per_page": 10,
  "current_page": 1,
  "last_page": 1,
  "data": [
    {
      "uid": "vib_2158d877e876",
      "object": "virtual_iban",
      "account_holder": "Platform by Online Payments Foundation",
      "iban": "DE0123456789098765",
      "bic": "DEUTDEFFVAC",
      "country": "DE",
      "currency": "EUR"
    },
    ...
  ]
}

    You can retrieve a list of all VIBAN available for you as a partner. The following filters can be used when retrieving VIBAN:

    Filter name Description
    country Shows VIBAN with a specific country.
    currency Shows VIBAN with a specific currency.

    Update Virtual IBAN

    Example request - Update viban object for the merchant:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}} \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
-d notify_url=https://platform.example.com/notify

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}
{
  "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify"
}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{viban_uid}}",
    "object": "virtual_iban",
    "created": 1674561507,
    "updated": 1674561507,
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify",
    "payment_details": {
        "provider_bank_account_name": "Platform name by Online Payments Foundation",
        "provider_bank_account_iban": "DE123456789123456",
        "provider_bank_account_bic": "DEUTDEFFVAC",
        "company_name": "Online Payments Foundation",
        "company_address_line_1": "Kanaalweg 1",
        "company_address_line_2": null,
        "company_zipcode": "2628EB",
        "company_city": "Delft",
        "company_country": "Netherlands",
        "bank_name": "Deutsche Bank AG",
        "bank_address_line_1": "Taunusanlage 12",
        "bank_address_line_2": null,
        "bank_zipcode": "60325",
        "bank_city": "Frankfurt am Main",
        "bank_country": "Germany"
    }
}

    You are able to update a VIBAN by performing a POST on the viban object. You can update one variable at a time, or multiple as you desire. The following fields can be updated:

    Variable Description
    notify_url Notification url to receive status updates.
    up to 255 characters

    Delete Virtual IBAN

    Example request - Delete VIBAN:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}} \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
-X DELETE

    DELETE https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}
    Please note that deleting a viban will return a 204 No Content!

    Whenever a VIBAN is deleted, funds transferred towards it will automatically be reversed to the buyer. Please note that the IBAN will be recycled after a certain period of time.

    Sandbox Simulation - Virtual IBAN

    Example request - Update the merchant bank_account status

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}/register-transaction \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
-d amount=100 \
-d "description=Test transaction VIBAN"

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/merchants/{{merchant_uid}}/profiles/{{profile_uid}}/virtual_ibans/{{viban_uid}}/register-transaction
{
  "amount": 100,
  "description": "Test transaction VIBAN"
}

    To register a transaction on a merchant profile's VIBAN, please use the endpoint as described on the right.

    Register transaction parameters
    amount required transaction amount
    description required transaction description

    Issuers

    OPP has several features that imply a connection with bank providers (also called issuers). For each of these features, it is necessary to obtain a list stipulating which banks support this feature. These lists rarely change, so it's therefore inefficient to continuously call external APIs. We therefore ask you to only retrieve the issuers once a day, and save them in a cache. This also makes the payment process faster on your end since your customers do not need to wait for the list to be loaded.

    PI-Single issuers

    Example request - Retrieve PI-Single issuers:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/payment-initiation-issuers \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/payment-initiation-issuers

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/payment-initiation-issuers",
    "has_more": true,
    "total_item_count": 310,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 31,
    "data": [
        {
            "object": "issuer",
            "country": "NLD",
            "bic": "RABONL2U",
            "name": "Issuer Simulation - Rabobank"
        },
        {
            "object": "issuer",
            "country": "NLD",
            "bic": "ABNANL2A",
            "name": "Issuer Simulation - ABN Amro"
        },
        {
            "object": "issuer",
            "country": "NLD",
            "bic": "INGBNL2A",
            "name": "Issuer Simulation - ING"
        },
        ...
    ]
}

    PI-Single issuers can be used for transactions that require PI-Single. When providing an issuer, the user of the platform will not be redirected to whitelabel pages of OPP, but will be redirected to his/her bank immediately. Do not forget to provide the country in the transaction request as well.

    This list rarely changes, so it's not efficient for you to keep calling external API's. We therefore ask you to only retrieve the issuers once a day, and save them in a cache. This also makes the payment process faster on your end since your customers do not need to wait for the list to be loaded.

    PI-Single issuers can be used for the following functionality:

    • Transactions

    iDIN issuers

    Example request - Retrieve iDIN issuers:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/idin_issuers \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/idin_issuers

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/idin_issuers",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 2,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "object": "idin_issuer",
            "bic": "INGBNL2A",
            "name": "ING Bank"
        },
        {
            "object": "idin_issuer",
            "bic": "RABONL2U",
            "name": "Rabobank"
        }
    ]
}

    iDIN issuers can be used for contact verification. When providing an issuer, the user of the platform will not be redirected to whitelabel pages of OPP, but will be redirected to his/her bank immediately. To do this, append the contact verification URL with {{verification_url}}?verification_type=idin&issuer={{BIC}}

    This list rarely changes, so it's not efficient for you to keep calling external API's. We therefore ask you to only retrieve the issuers once a day, and save them in a cache. This also makes the payment process faster on your end since your customers do not need to wait for the list to be loaded.

    iDIN issuers can be used for the following functionality:

    • Contact verification

    eMandate issuers

    Example request - Retrieve iDEAL issuers:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/mandate_issuers \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/mandates/mandate_issuers

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/mandates/mandate_issuers",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 2,
    "items_per_page": 25,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "object": "mandate_issuer",
            "bic": "INGBNL2A",
            "name": "Core issuer simulation - ING",
            "mandate_type": "consumer"
        },
        {
            "object": "mandate_issuer",
            "bic": "INGBNL2A",
            "name": "B2B issuer simulation",
            "mandate_type": "business"
        }
    ]
}

    eMandate issuers can be used for mandate verification. When providing an issuer, the user of the platform will not be redirected to whitelabel pages of OPP, but will be redirected to his/her bank immediately.

    This list rarely changes, so it's not efficient for you to keep calling external API's. We therefore ask you to only retrieve the issuers once a day, and save them in a cache. This also makes the payment process faster on your end since your customers do not need to wait for the list to be loaded.

    eMandate issuers can be used for the following functionality:

    • Mandate verification

    Mandate issuers can be filtered on mandate_type. This can either be business or consumer. Use a filter as: v1/mandates/mandate_issuers?filter[mandate_type]=consumer

    Disputes

    Disputes can be created for transactions in escrow. If a dispute is created for a transaction while transaction is in escrow the transaction will not go to completed state when the escrow period has passed.

    The transaction will be refunded when a dispute is closed in favor of the contact (buyer). The transaction will be settled when a dispute is closed in favor of the merchant.

    The use of Disputes can only be activated after a signed support contract, including dispute handling. Please ask your sales manager for more information.

    This chapter will provide you all the necessary information about and functionality of a dispute object. We already provided a Postman collection on the Disputes API for you to start with.

    Object Definition - Dispute

    The Dispute object contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    uid string Dispute unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    dispute
    created timestamp Timestamp when dispute is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when dispute is updated.
    closed timestamp Timestamp when dispute is completed.
    closed_in_favor_of string To whom the dispute was closed in favor of.
    one of:
    merchant
    contact
    reference string Reference for the dispute. This will be a UID from the OPP system.
    status string Current status of the object.
    one of:
    createdpendingon_holdresolvedirrelevant
    reason string Reason for the dispute.
    one of:
    defaultcancellationfraudinternalcomplaintproduct_has_damageother
    reason_codes array Reason codes belonging to the dispute. See Dispute Reason Codes
    contact object Contains:

    name_first
    string

    First name of the contact.

    name_last
    string

    Last name of the contact.

    emailaddress
    string

    Email address of the contact.

    phonenumber
    string

    Phonenumber of the contact.
    tickets array Tickets belonging to the dispute. Will always show 2 tickets, for merchant and for contact. See Dispute Tickets
    messages array Messages belonging to the dispute. See Dispute Messages
    notify_url string Notification url to receive status updates.
    up to 255 characters
    mediation_flow array Mediation flow information. null if no mediation is used. See Dispute Mediation Flow
    metadata array Set of key / value pairs to store additional data.

    Dispute Reason Codes Definition

    Reason Code Description
    710 T&T: Package not received
    711 T&T: package on the way
    720 Cancellation: by buyer
    721 Cancellation: by seller
    722 Cancellation: seller doesn't want to use service
    723 Cancellation: KYC failed
    724 Cancellation: product already sold
    725 Cancellation: product broken before sending
    726 Cancellation: Pick up
    730 Product not as described
    731 Product not as described: big deviation
    732 Product not as described: small deviation
    733 Product not as described: fake product
    740 Buyer returned product without confirmation
    750 Suspected fraud: Empty box
    760 Carrier lost package
    761 Product broken by delivery
    780 No dispute reason
    770 Price: Change amount

    Dispute Status

    The dispute status is a status indication about the dispute in the OPP system. According to this status, you should handle the flow on your platform.

    The possible status flows can be found below:

    Status Explanation
    created Dispute is created, no action has taken place yet.
    pending Dispute is pending and currently under investigation.
    on_hold Dispute is currently on hold. This may happen if OPP requires further information from the merchant or buyer.
    resolved Dispute is resolved. The transaction has been updated to ‘completed’ or ‘refunded’ status based on the dispute’s outcome.
    irrelevant Dispute is irrelevant or not part of the escrow services. Information may be used for fraud-detection and such.

    Notification Example - Dispute

    Example notification dispute status:

    {
  "uid": "{{notification_uid}}",
  "type": "dispute.status.changed",
  "created": 1684932083,
  "object_uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
  "object_type": "dispute",
  "object_url": "https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes/{{dispute_uid}}",
  "verification_hash": "{{hash}}"
}

    Please find a notification example to the right.

    Create Dispute

    Example request - Create dispute:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}"
    -d reference={{transaction_uid}} \
    -d reason=complaint \
    -d "message=product not received within 14 days." \
    -d "contact[name_first]=John" \
    -d "contact[name_last]=Tester" \
    -d "contact[emailaddress]=john@domain.com" \
    -d notify_url=ttps://platform.example.com/notify/"
    -d metadata[external_id]=2015486

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes
{
    "reference": "{{transaction_uid}}",
    "reason": "complaint",
    "message": "product not received within 14 days",
    "contact": {
        "name_first": "John",
        "name_last": "Tester",
        "emailaddress": "john@domain.com"
    },
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify/",
    "metadata":
      {
        "external_id": "2015486"
      }
}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
    "object": "dispute",
    "created": 1604406605,
    "updated": 1614118004,
    "closed": null,
    "closed_in_favor_of": null,
    "reference": "{{transaction_uid}}",
    "status": "created",
    "reason": "complaint",
    "reason_codes": [],
    "contact": {
        "name_first": "John",
