        "name_last": "Tester",
        "emailaddress": "john@domain.com",
        "phonenumber": null
    },
    "tickets": [],
    "messages": [],
    "notify_url": "",
    "mediation_flow": null,
    "metadata": []
}

    You can create a Dispute using the Disputes API. Below are all dispute object variables that can be used in the POST request when creating a dispute.

    Variable Description
    reference required Reference UID the dispute is about
    reason required Reason for the dispute.
    one of:
    fraudcomplaintother
    message required Short message that explains the reason for the dispute.
    up to 255 characters
    contact required Contact person that created the dispute.

    name_first
    required

    First name of the contact.

    name_last
    required

    Last name of the contact.

    emailaddress
    required

    Email address of the contact.

    phonenumber
    required

    Phonenumber of the contact.
    notify_url required Notification url to receive status updates
    up to 255 characters
    metadata object with key-value pairs Additional data that belongs to the transaction object.

    Retrieve Dispute

    Example request - Retrieve a single dispute object:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes/{{dispute_uid}} \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes/{{dispute_uid}}

    Example response:

    {
    "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
    "object": "dispute",
    "created": 1604406605,
    "updated": 1614118004,
    "closed": null,
    "closed_in_favor_of": null,
    "reference": "{{transaction_uid}}",
    "status": "created",
    "reason": "complaint",
    "reason_codes": [],
    "contact": {
        "name_first": "John",
        "name_last": "Tester",
        "emailaddress": "john@domain.com",
        "phonenumber": null
    },
    "tickets": [],
    "messages": [],
    "notify_url": "",
    "mediation_flow": null,
    "metadata": []
}

    Example request - Retrieve all disputes:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes

    Example response:

    {
    "object": "list",
    "url": "/v1/disputes",
    "has_more": false,
    "total_item_count": 3,
    "items_per_page": 10,
    "current_page": 1,
    "last_page": 1,
    "data": [
        {
            "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
            "object": "dispute",
            "created": 1604406605,
            "updated": 1614118004,
            "closed": null,
            "closed_in_favor_of": null,
            "reference": "{{transaction_uid}}",
            "status": "created",
            "reason": "complaint",
            "contact": {
                "name_first": "John",
                "name_last": "Tester",
                "emailaddress": "john@domain.com",
                "phonenumber": null
            },
            "tickets": [],
            "messages": [],
            "notify_url": "",
            "mediation_flow": null,
            "metadata": []
        },
        ...
    ]
}

    You can retrieve an individual dispute as well as a list of all disputes. The following filters can be used when retrieving disputes:

    Filter name Description
    status Shows disputes with a specific status.
    reason Shows disputes with a specific reason.
    date_created Only in extended filter. Shows the disputes within a specific created date.

    Dispute Reason Codes

    Example response - Expanding reason codes:

    {
  "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
  "object": "dispute",
  "created": 1604406605,
  "updated": 1614118004,
  ...
  "reason_codes": [
    {
      "created": 1678204294,
      "code": 710,
      "description": "Track and Trace: Package not received
  ]
  "tickets": [],
  "messages": [],
  "notify_url": "",
  "mediation_flow": null,
  "metadata": []
}

    When expanding certain objects, they will appear when requested. Objects that do not have any CRUD functionality, but are either set by OPP or only set when creating the transaction can be found here.

    Variable Description
    created timestamp Timestamp when dispute reason code is created.
    code integer Reason code as defined in Dispute Reason Codes.
    description string Description of the reason code

    Dispute Messages

    Example response - Expanding messages:

    {
    "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
    "object": "dispute",
    "created": 1604406605,
    "updated": 1614118004,
    ...
    "tickets": [],
    "messages": [
        {
            "uid": "{{dispute_message_uid}}",
            "object": "dispute_message",
            "created": 1600417804,
            "updated": 1600417804,
            "author": "external",
            "message": "product not received within 14 days."
        }
    ],
    "notify_url": "",
    "mediation_flow": null,
    "metadata": []
}
    Variable Description
    uid string Dispute Message unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    dispute_message
    created timestamp Timestamp when dispute is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when dispute is updated.
    author string Author of the message.
    one of:
    internal - OPP
    external - Contact, Merchant or Partner
    message string Message text.

    Dispute Mediation Flow

    Example response - Expanding mediation flow:

    {
  "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
  "object": "dispute",
  "created": 1604406605,
  "updated": 1614118004,
  ...
  "notify_url": "",
  "mediation_flow": {
    "uid": "mfl_d91da8f82dc9",
    "created": 1666788156,
    "updated": 1666788230,
    "status": "review",
    "auto_mediation": false,
    "closed_by_user_type": null,
    "object": "mediation_flow",
    "statuses": [
      {
        "created": 1635952204,
        "name": "created"
      },
      {
        "created": 1635952204,
        "name": "pending"
      },
      {
        "created": 1635952204,
        "name": "awaiting"
      },
      {
        "created": 1635952204,
        "name": "review"
      }
    ]
  },
  "metadata": []
}
    Variable Description
    uid string Dispute Mediation unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    created timestamp Timestamp when dispute mediation is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when dispute mediation is updated.
    status string Status of the mediation. Nullable.
    One of:
    created - The mediation has been created
    pending - Contact started a mediation but did not complete it yet. Merchant will be paid once the escrow period ends
    awaiting - Payment is stopped by the Contact. The mediation is in progress between Contact and Merchant.
    review - Contact and Merchant did not get to an agreement. OPP will review the transaction.
    closed - The mediation has ended.
    auto_mediation boolean Whether or not mediation was closed without review of OPP.
    closed_by_user_type string Closed by.
    At least one of:
    admin
    contact
    merchant
    partner
    system
    object string Value:
    mediation_flow
    statuses array Statuses of the mediation flow.

    Dispute Tickets

    Example response - Expanding tickets:

    {
    "uid": "{{dispute_uid}}",
    "object": "dispute",
    "created": 1604406605,
    "updated": 1614118004,
    ...
    "tickets": {
                "contact": {
                    "uid": "{{ticket_uid}}",
                    "object": "ticket",
                    "created": 1597738468,
                    "updated": 1623663025,
                    "category": "dispute",
                    "status": "awaiting",
                    "token": "{{token}}",
                    "subject": "{{dispute_uid}} - Contact",
                    "url": "https://sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/nl/public/support/{{ticket_uid}}/b45f89b7fc49e2fd894441697a2abb892c2c2fa3"
                },
                "merchant": {
                    "uid": "{{ticket_uid}}",
                    "object": "ticket",
                    "created": 1597738469,
                    "updated": 1623663025,
                    "category": "dispute",
                    "status": "closed",
                    "token": "{{token}}",
                    "subject": "{{dispute_uid}} - Merchant",
                    "url": "https://sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/nl/public/support/{{ticket_uid}}/4d5893fc159d23ba88879f1b86955f51774bc2f4"
                }
            },
    "messages": [],
    "notify_url": "",
    "metadata": []
}
    Variable Description
    uid string Ticket unique identifier.
    up to 20 characters
    object string Value:
    ticket
    created timestamp Timestamp when ticket is created.
    updated timestamp Timestamp when ticket is updated.
    category string Category of the dispute.
    one of:
    paymentfraudgeneraldisputecontact
    status string Current status of the object.
    one of:
    openrepliedonholdawaitingclosed
    token string Ticket token.
    subject string Ticket subject.
    url string redirect URL to the ticket.

    Update Dispute

    Currently, it is not possible to update a dispute using our APIs

    Delete Dispute

    Currently, it is not possible to delete a dispute using our APIs

    Sandbox Simulation - Dispute

    Example request - Trigger a dispute status:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes/{{dispute_uid}}/messages \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
    -d "message=resolved"

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/disputes/{{dispute_uid}}/messages
{
    "message": "resolved"
}

    Use the following messages to trigger dispute statuses on sandbox mode.

    Message Status
    pending pending
    on_hold on_hold
    resolved resolved, closed in favor of merchant
    resolved-merchant resolved, closed in favor of merchant
    resolved-contact resolved, closed in favor of contact
    irrelevant irrelevant, closed in favor of merchant

    Partner

    As a partner, you have the possibility to change certain partner specific configurations via the API as well. This might come in handy when you have your own backoffice or would like to work with different roles in the company.

    This section shows the possibilities of the Partner API.

    Configurations

    This chapter will provide you all the necessary information about Configurations and the functionalities that go with it. The configurations endpoint allows you to change any configuration via the API. Please keep in mind that this endpoint is still under development.

    We have already provided a Postman collection on the Partner API for you to start with.

    Object Definition - Configuration

    The Configuration object contains the following variables.

    Variables Description
    notify_url string Notification url to receive status updates for this merchant.
    up to 255 characters, nullable

    Create Configuration

    Currently, it is not possible to create configurations via the API.

    Retrieve Configuration

    Example request - Retrieve all configurations:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/configuration \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \

    GET https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/configuration

    Example response:

    {
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify"
}

    You can retrieve a list of all configurations available for you as a partner. The following filters can be used when retrieving configurations:

    Filter name Description
    Currently, no filters are available for configurations.

    Update Configuration

    Example request - Update configuration:

    curl https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/configuration \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}" \
-d notify_url=https://platform.example.com/notify

    POST https://api-sandbox.onlinebetaalplatform.nl/v1/configuration
{
  "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify"
}

    Example response:

    {
    "notify_url": "https://platform.example.com/notify"
}

    You are able to update a configuration by performing a POST on the configuration endpoint. You can update one variable at a time, or multiple as you desire. The following fields can be updated:

    Variable Description
    notify_url Notification url to receive status updates.
    up to 255 characters. Nullable

    Delete Configurations

    Currently it is not possible to delete Configurations via the API.

    Changelog

    This changelog will contain differences made to the online docs, collected by year and week. Providing you the possibility to read back everything that has been changed since the last time you visited this page.

    2025

    Here you can find all changes to the API documentation from 2024, ordered per week.

    Week 31

    • Add Right to work json requests examples

    Week 27

    • Add right to work documentation
    • Add timestamp for compliance requirements
    • Add statuses to refund

    Week 7

    • Add paysafecard to Payment Methods, Transaction and Multi Transaction
    • Remove all iDEAL V1 issuer options from the API

    2024

    Here you can find all changes to the API documentation from 2024, ordered per week.

    Week 46

    • Add sepa_reference to Single Transaction, Multi Transaction and Multi Transaction Import

    Week 43

    • Fixed an inconsistency in the Virtual IBAN notification response.

    Week 40

    • Add Merchant Balance object that allows for balance breakdown per merchant and per profile.
    • Remove continuous settlement limitation from Profile Balance object

    Week 34

    • Remove deprecated payment methods Giropay, Sofort and PayPal-PC
    • Deprecate iDEAL Issuers
    • Add new receiver_details fields to Withdrawals

    Week 32

    • Add Configurations for Partners.

    Week 28

    • Add buyer_uid to Transactions endpoint

    Week 18

    • Add Apple Pay as a payment method.

    Week 17

    • Add Currencies and minor units to API Basics.
    • Remove partner onboarding form link.

    Week 13

    • Add date_expired property to the update Transactions and update Multi Transaction endpoints.
    • Add return_url, notify_url, currency and payment_link_recipients property to the Payment Links.
    • change return_url property to be optional in create Payment Links.

    Week 7

    • Add currency to Merchant Charge object definition and Partner Charge object definition.
    • Add currency to Mandate object definition.
    • Add currency to Settlement object definition.
    • Add currency to Withdrawal object definition.
    • Add currency to Payment link object definition.
    • Add currency to Refund object definition.
    • Add currency to Multi Transaction object definition.
    • Add currency to Multi Transaction Import object definition.

    Week 6

    • Add shareholder_percentage property to the create UBO and update UBO endpoints.
    • Add decision_maker_percentage property to the create UBO and update UBO endpoints.

    Week 2

    • Add Virtual IBAN api endpoints for merchant profiles.
    • Add PayPal (paypal-ppcp) as a new payment method for Transactions and Multi-Transactions.
    • Add disapproval reason codes for Bank Accounts, Contacts, CoC, Organization Structure and UBO.
    • Add `deleted` status to Merchant Status for GDPR deleted merchants.

    2023

    Here you can find all changes to the API documentation from 2023, ordered per week.

    Week 40

    • Add Payment Links.
    • Add idnow as one of the possible verified_with methods in Merchant Contacts.

    Week 32

    • Add merchant.profile.status.changed Notification types.

    Week 31

    • Add virtual_ibans and risk_level_weight to Profile object definition.
    • Add Delete Profile functionality.

    Week 28

    • Add Profile Status.
    • Add update status to Update Profile request.

    Week 26

    • Add possibility to Delete Withdrawal in status on-hold.
    • Add Order object variables.

    Week 25

    • Add disapproval reasons to consumer merchant's bank account object.
    • Add disapproval reasons to consumer merchant's contact object.

    Week 24

    • Add new IP address 18.159.20.179 to Sandbox Environment.

    Week 23

    • Add Dispute mediation flow to Dispute.

    Week 21

    • Add new way to validate notifications: by using signed notification headers.
    • Add notification type: merchant.compliance_requirement.created.
    • Add notification type: virtual_iban.transaction.registered.
    • Add example notification profile balance.
    • Add example notification dispute.
    • Add example notification mandate.
    • Add example notification payment reference.
    • Add frontend security limitation.

    Week 19

    • Add DELETE Multi Transaction functionality.
    • Add Withdrawal example notification.

    Week 11

    • Add Expanding objects possibility on POST requests.
    • Add GO-LIVE requirement for partner backoffice.

    Week 10

    • Add sandbox simulation for merchant tickets.
    • Add Dispute Reason Codes. It is also added to response object of Disputes.

    Week 4

    • Add legal entity list to be provided in legal_entity when creating a business merchant.

    Week 1

    • Add virtual_iban_uid to Create Payment Reference.
    • You can now Update an UBO via the API.
    • Add source_of_funds.required to Compliance Requirements.

    2022

    Here you can find all changes to the API documentation from 2022, ordered per week.

    Week 48

    • Add merchant UID and profile UID to Create Multi Transaction.
    • Add Retrieve Multi Transaction Transactions.

    Week 46

    • Add Virtual IBAN Object.
    • Add virtual_iban_uid to Create Transaction and Create Multi Transaction.
    • Add retrieve transactions endpoint for >20 transactions within Retrieve Multi Transaction

    Week 37

    • Add Merchant Tickets Object.

    Week 36

    • Change Gender x to other in Contacts and Mandates.
    • Add is_pep variable to Create Merchant and Create Contact.
    • Add note on restricted countries for Mandate Methods.

    Week 29

    • Add 409 conflict response.
    • Add continuous as a possibility for settlement_interval in the Create Merchant.

    Week 23

    • Remove deprecated simulation amounts.

    Week 21

    • Add Multi Transaction Import and the new Notification that goes along with it.
    • Add several Notification Examples to Objects.

    Week 16

    • Change Withdrawals API to accept execution_date and new statuses.
    • Add new ethics form and checklists for go-live.
    • Add bank account details to Create Bank Account when using Files API.
    • It is now possible to change the partner_fee when updating a Transaction in escrow.

    Week 13

    • Add escrow_period and escrow_date as optional variables for Mandate Transactions.

    Week 9

    • Add payout_description for Charges API.

    Week 7

    • Profiles API available to create and update profiles.
    • Add Sandbox Simulation for Compliance Requirements.
    • Add Sandbox Simulation for UBO object.

    Week 4

    • Add Disputes API.

    2021

    Here you can find all changes to the API documentation from 2021, ordered per week.

    Week 50

    • Created Withdrawals Postman collection.
    • Created Charges Postman collection.
    • Updated Mandates Postman collection.
    • Updated Merchants Postman collection.

    Week 49

    • Add status update in Merchant API.
    • Updating or deleting Metadata is now available everywhere where metadata is available.
    • Updated Transactions Postman collection.
    • Add PI-Single Issuers.
    • Add country to Create Transactions for pi-single payments.

    Week 47

    • Add new from and to UIDs to Charges API and include filter.

    Week 46

    • Change sandbox simulation amounts for mandates, mandate (multi) transactions, (multi) transactions and withdrawals.
    • Add limitations due to Security reasons.

    Week 44

    • Update UBO object with share and country_of_residence.
    • Add coc_extract and organization_structure to Files API.

    Week 41

    • Change Merchant Status flow to not allow suspended -> live.

    Week 39

    • Add Files API chapter to allow seamless KYC.

    Week 38

    • Rewrite Charges chapter to align with CRUD.
    • Add settlement_interval to Create Merchant.
    • Add filters to Multi transaction retrieval.

    Week 32

    • Add Withdrawals to API documentation.
    • Add limitations on notifications.

    Week 31

    • Add Go-Live list for production.

    Week 30

    • Partial refund on escrow payments is now possible via API.
    • Change Mandate Multi Transaction chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Moved sandbox simulations to their own sections within object chapter.

    Week 28

    • Add iDIN simulation.

    Week 27

    • Change Mandate chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Mandate Transaction chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Add Mandate payment details with reasoncodes.

    Week 26

    • Transaction notify_url can be updated on Transaction API.

    Week 25

    • Add Payment Reference.

    Week 22

    • Add verified_with to Bank account and Merchant Contact.

    Week 19

    • Add default locale to Merchant create.

    Week 18

    • Add max characters to payout_description Refund creation.
    • Add Giropay simulations.

    Week 14

    • Add new Sandbox IP address 18.193.11.50.

    Week 12

    • Add Payment Details definition to transactions.
    • Change Transaction chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Multi Transaction chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Refund chapter to align with CRUD layout.

    Week 7

    • Add curl example of authentication header.
    • Change authentication of all curl examples to Bearer Token.
    • Add license information page DNB to merchant countries.
    • Add name_first and name_last in merchant create request.
    • Change Merchants chapter to align with CRUD layout; add delete and update merchant information.
    • Change Addresses chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Contacts chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Compliance chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change UBOs chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Bank Accounts chapter to align with CRUD layout.
    • Change Profiles chapter to align with CRUD layout.

    Week 3

    • Add API status endpoint.
    • Add planned status to Transactions API.
    • Add Sandbox phone verification explanation.
    • Add Mandate filter to mandate transactions.
    • Remove deprecated Signup API sandbox simulations.

    • Change Phonenumber requirement to align compliance process

    • Change Partner charges endpoint

    • Remove Signup API
